query: Yes, please go ahead and make the swap to the cheapest Desk Lamp and put the $17.99 refund on a gift card. That’s the only change I want to make to this order.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user wants to modify order W9300146 by replacing the Desk Lamp with the cheapest Desk Lamp variant and wants any refund on a gift card.
assistant: The assistant authenticates the user, retrieves the pending order, and fetches the Desk Lamp product variants.
assistant: The assistant explains that the current lamp costs $153.23, the cheapest available variant costs $135.24, calculates a $17.99 refund, and asks for confirmation while reminding the user to confirm this is the only item change.
user: The user confirms the cheapest-variant swap, the gift-card refund destination, and that no other items need modification.

candidate_answers.text: Thanks for confirming. I’ll update order W9300146 to the cheapest available Desk Lamp and apply the $17.99 refund to your gift card now.
The user has provided explicit confirmation for a single pending-order item swap plus the refund method, so the grounded next move is the modify-items tool.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a state where the customer has confirmed the exact replacement variant, refund destination, and completeness of requested item changes, making the item-modification action ready to execute.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from visible state. Scores prioritize the single allowed pending-order item modification after explicit confirmation over repeated lookup or unrelated order operations.
notes: query comes from the final user confirmation immediately preceding the modify_pending_order_items tool call
history_summary includes only already-visible order and product facts needed to justify the action
best_action is argmax and consistent with retail policy requiring confirmation before database updates
this sample adds a retail item-modification decision point distinct from simple lookup and address editing
